headline: Apple Stock Hit a Record High. Why June Could Change Everything.

headline: This Sneaky AI Play Nearly Doubled Sales. The Stock Is Rising Sharply.
summary: Shares of  SiTime  spiked Thursday after the maker of timing solutions nearly doubled revenue from last year, reflecting strong demand from artificial-intelligence data centers.  After the closing bell Wednesday, SiTime reported adjusted earnings of $1.44 a share for the first quarter, up from 26 cents a year ago and well above analysts’ consensus estimates of $1.16.  The company introduced a full-year revenue growth forecast of at least 80%.

content.title: This Sneaky AI Play Nearly Doubled Sales. The Stock Is Rising Sharply.
content.summary: Shares of  SiTime  spiked Thursday after the maker of timing solutions nearly doubled revenue from last year, reflecting strong demand from artificial-intelligence data centers.  After the closing bell Wednesday, SiTime reported adjusted earnings of $1.44 a share for the first quarter, up from 26 cents a year ago and well above analysts’ consensus estimates of $1.16.  The company introduced a full-year revenue growth forecast of at least 80%.

headline: Rave files antitrust lawsuit against Apple over removal of video-sharing app
summary: Software developer Rave on Thursday filed an antitrust lawsuit against Apple, alleging that the iPhone maker ‌kicked Rave's app for shared viewing of video content off the Apple ‌App Store after introducing a competing product called SharePlay.  Ontario, Canada-based Rave is demanding reinstatement into the ​App Store and "hundreds of millions of dollars" in damages, according to the complaint filed in U.S. federal court in New Jersey.  Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

content.title: Rave files antitrust lawsuit against Apple over removal of video-sharing app
content.summary: Software developer Rave on Thursday filed an antitrust lawsuit against Apple, alleging that the iPhone maker ‌kicked Rave's app for shared viewing of video content off the Apple ‌App Store after introducing a competing product called SharePlay.  Ontario, Canada-based Rave is demanding reinstatement into the ​App Store and "hundreds of millions of dollars" in damages, according to the complaint filed in U.S. federal court in New Jersey.  Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

headline: Trump meets with Brazil's Lula to talk trade, extends EU tariff deadline
